---
title: "recipe 12 latitude"
output: html_document
date: "2026-05-30"
---

```{r}
# ── Recipe 12: Latitude Gradient ──────────────────────────────
source("https://michaelalfaro.github.io/eeb187-demos/recipes/code/helpers.R")

# ── 1. Load your data ────────────────────────────────────────
traits <- read.csv("/Users/Flo/Downloads/EEB187/traits_df_78.csv",
                    stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
bundle <- load_bundle("/Users/Flo/Downloads/EEB187/scarinae-78-bundle")
# Strip specimen codes from tree tips
bundle$tree$tip.label <- gsub("^([A-Za-z]+_[a-z]+).*", "\\1", bundle$tree$tip.label)

# Remove any duplicate tips created by stripping (keep first occurrence)
dups <- duplicated(bundle$tree$tip.label)
if (any(dups)) {
  cat("Dropping", sum(dups), "duplicate tips after stripping\n")
  bundle$tree <- ape::drop.tip(bundle$tree, bundle$tree$tip.label[dups])
}

# ── 2. Pull latitude data from FishBase ──────────────────────
# In rfishbase 5.x these columns are `Northernmost` / `Southermost`
# (older rfishbase used `NorthernLatitude` / `SouthernLatitude`).
fb <- pull_fishbase(traits$species,
                    fields = list(stocks = c("Northernmost",
                                             "Southermost")))

# ── 3. Compute midpoint latitude ─────────────────────────────

merged <- merge(traits, fb, by = "species")

# Force latitude columns to numeric (pull_fishbase may return them as character)
merged$Northernmost <- as.numeric(merged$Northernmost)
merged$Southermost  <- as.numeric(merged$Southermost)

merged <- merged[!is.na(merged$Northernmost) &
                  !is.na(merged$Southermost), ]
merged$abs_midlat <- abs((merged$Northernmost +
                           merged$Southermost) / 2)
merged <- merged[!is.na(merged$Northernmost) &
                  !is.na(merged$Southermost), ]
merged$abs_midlat <- abs((merged$Northernmost +
                           merged$Southermost) / 2)

cat("Species with latitude data:", nrow(merged), "\n")
cat("Midpoint latitude range:",
    round(range(merged$abs_midlat), 1), "\n")

# ── 4. Match to tree ────────────────────────────────────────

# Only pass species + pavo variables to match_tree_data
merged_slim <- merged[, c("species", "m", "A", "Jc", "Jt", "m_dS", "m_dL")]

matched <- match_tree_data(bundle$tree, merged_slim, species_col = "species")
tree    <- matched$tree
dat     <- matched$data

matched <- match_tree_data(bundle$tree, merged, species_col = "species")

# ── 5. PGLS: chromatic diversity vs latitude ─────────────────
model <- safe_pgls(Jc ~ abs_midlat, df = matched$data,
                    tree = matched$tree)

# ── 6. Scatter plot ─────────────────────────────────────────
library(ggplot2)

p <- ggplot(matched$data, aes(x = abs_midlat, y = Jc)) +
  geom_point(size = 4, alpha = 0.7, color = "#264653") +
  { if (!is.null(model))
    geom_abline(intercept = coef(model)[1], slope = coef(model)[2],
                color = "#e76f51", linewidth = 1.2) } +
  labs(x = "Absolute Midpoint Latitude (degrees from equator)",
       y = "Chromatic Diversity (Jc)",
       title = "Does latitude predict chromatic diversity?",
       subtitle = "PGLS controlling for phylogeny") +
  theme_poster

print(p)
ggsave("results/recipe12-latitude-vs-Jc.pdf", p, width = 10, height = 8)
cat("Saved: results/recipe12-latitude-vs-Jc.pdf\n")
```
```{r}
# Check what's non-numeric
str(merged)

# Or just see which columns are character type
colnames(merged)[sapply(merged, is.character)]
```
